summarization.a: The figure displays a series of X-ray diffraction patterns for PVD Ti/PE-ALD Co/Si(001) at different annealing temperatures (T_a). Peaks corresponding to CoSi_2 (200) and β-Co (111) are observed.

a.question: What annealing temperatures are required to have CoSi2 (200)?
What is different to the material with an annealing temperature of 400 degrees with respect to all other temperatures?
a.answer: a	400 degrees annealing is the only process that shows two crystalline phases, indicating a polycristalline film.

a.question: At 700 degrees annealing temperature, is there any crystalline material?
a.answer: a	Eventhough the spectrum does not show this, there can still be crystalline material for higher angles.

a.question: What is the dominent crystalline phase for an annealing temperature of 600 degrees?
